title: Fix PostgreSQL Enum error in Admin Stats
description: The `/api/v1/admin/stats` endpoint is failing with a PostgreSQL error `invalid input value for enum orderstatus:

`. This is caused by using string literals in a query filter instead of the proper `OrderStatus` enum members. Your task is to fix `backend/app/api/v1/admin/router.py` by importing `OrderStatus` from `backend/app/db/models/order.py` and using its members (e.g., `OrderStatus.paid`) in the `paid_statuses` list. Additionally, you should uncomment and restore the `top_products` aggregation logic which was likely disabled incorrectly during debugging.
acceptance_criteria: The `paid_statuses` list must use `OrderStatus` enum members, not strings.
The commented-out code for `top_products` calculation is restored and operational.
After the fix, a GET request to `/api/v1/admin/stats` (with admin privileges) must return a 200 OK status.
The endpoint correctly returns statistics including `total_revenue` and `top_products`.
